name: Tofu Meatloaf
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup olive oil (for sauteing the above)
4 cups bread crumbs (I use whatever left - wheat, Italian; the more kinds, the better)
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute the chopped onion, garlic, and olive oil in a sauté skillet (at least 8 inches) until glassy.
2. Add green pepper, mushrooms, and half of the Newman's dressing. Reduce heat and continue to saute until peppers are cooked (no longer crunchy).
3. Add seasoning to personal preference. Preheat oven to 425°F.
4. In a large bowl, combine bread crumbs, one can of tomato paste, cheeses, and tofu. Beat the egg separately and add it to the tofu-bread mixture. Combine until bread is thoroughly saturated.
5. Add sauteed vegetables and remaining Newman's dressing. The meatloaf mixture should be cohesive enough to form into a loaf but not soggy. If too soggy, add more bread. If too crumbly, add more dressing or tomato paste.
6. Spray a loaf pan with non-stick cooking spray. Spoon the tofu mixture into the pan and spread evenly.
7. Spoon remaining can of tomato paste over the tofu mixture and spread evenly on top.
8. Bake in the 425°F oven for 15-25 minutes, or until juices bubble up around the edges.
